meta.title: Circular Dichroism Studies on Southern Bean Mosaic Virus

abstractHtml: Circular dichroism spectra showed that the RNA of southern bean mosaic virus (SBMV) has considerable base pairing and that the protein subunit has a higher proportion of -structure than of α-helix. A specific feature of the circular dichroism (CD) spectra of SBMV at 230 nm appears to be related to the binding of divalent cations to the virion; Ca2+ affects this CD spectral feature at a lower concentration than does Mg2+ or Sr2+. The CD spectra are compared with those of turnip rosette virus (TRosV), another member of the SBMV group.
